 {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-abl","title":"Auto-detect workers from SSH config and aliases","description":"## Overview
Automatically discover potential worker machines from user's SSH config (~/.ssh/config) and shell aliases (e.g., alias css='ssh ...'), then generate or update workers.toml.

## Background
In this dogfooding session, the user had SSH aliases 'css' and 'csd' defined. These had to be manually extracted and converted to workers.toml format. This pattern is common - users often have SSH shortcuts for their machines.

## Requirements
1. Parse ~/.ssh/config for Host entries with HostName/User/IdentityFile
2. Parse ~/.bashrc, ~/.zshrc for ssh alias patterns like: alias x='ssh -i key user@host'
3. Present discovered hosts to user for selection
4. Optionally probe selected hosts for core count (nproc)
5. Generate workers.toml entries with sensible defaults (slots = cores - 16)
6. Support --interactive mode for guided setup
7. Support --auto mode that adds all discovered hosts

## Technical Approach
- Regex patterns for SSH config parsing (Host, HostName, User, IdentityFile)
- Regex for shell alias: alias X='ssh (-i KEY)? (USER@)?HOST'
- Deduplicate hosts found in multiple places
- Use existing SSH infrastructure to probe hosts
- Generate TOML using toml crate's serialization

## Edge Cases
- Hosts with only IP, no domain name
- Multiple identity files for same host
- Hosts behind jump servers (ProxyJump)
- Wildcard Host patterns (Host *)
- Aliases using environment variables

## Success Criteria
- rch workers discover finds css and csd from aliases
- rch workers discover --auto adds them to workers.toml
- rch workers discover --interactive lets user pick","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T02:16:31.080324437-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T03:32:21.368318714-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T03:32:21.368318714-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented workers discover command with SSH config and shell alias parsing, --probe for connectivity testing, and --add --yes for auto-adding workers. Interactive selection deferred to future enhancement."}

 {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-aeq","title":"Add BunTest and BunTypecheck to CompilationKind Enum","description":"# Task: Add Bun CompilationKind Variants

## Overview

Extend the `CompilationKind` enum to include variants for Bun commands. This enables the classifier to categorize Bun commands with the same precision as Rust commands.

## What This Task Does

1. Add `BunTest` variant to `CompilationKind` enum
2. Add `BunTypecheck` variant to `CompilationKind` enum
3. Ensure serialization/deserialization works correctly
4. Update any exhaustive matches

## Technical Details

### File: rch-common/src/patterns.rs

**Change 1: Add enum variants (line ~85)**
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
pub enum CompilationKind {
    // Rust commands
    CargoBuild,
    CargoTest,
    CargoCheck,
    CargoClippy,
    CargoDoc,
    Rustc,
    
    // C/C++ commands
    Gcc,
    Gpp,
    Clang,
    Clangpp,
    
    // Build systems
    Make,
    CmakeBuild,
    Ninja,
    Meson,
    
    // Bun commands (NEW)
    /// bun test - Runs Bun's built-in test runner
    BunTest,
    /// bun typecheck - Runs TypeScript type checking
    BunTypecheck,
}
```

## Design Decisions

### Why Separate Variants?
- `BunTest` and `BunTypecheck` have different semantics
- May need different worker selection (test workers vs type-check workers)
- Enables fine-grained metrics and logging
- Future: different confidence thresholds per kind

### Why Not BunGeneric?
- Generic variants reduce precision
- We want explicit control over what's intercepted
- Makes never-intercept logic clearer
- Easier to add new commands (BunLint, etc.) later

### Serialization
The `#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]` attribute ensures:
- `BunTest` → "bun_test" in JSON
- `BunTypecheck` → "bun_typecheck" in JSON
- Consistent with existing variants

## Testing Requirements

```rust
#[test]
fn test_bun_compilation_kind_serde() {
    // BunTest
    let kind = CompilationKind::BunTest;
    let json = serde_json::to_string(&kind).unwrap();
    assert_eq!(json, "\"bun_test\"");
    let parsed: CompilationKind = serde_json::from_str(&json).unwrap();
    assert_eq!(parsed, CompilationKind::BunTest);
    
    // BunTypecheck
    let kind = CompilationKind::BunTypecheck;
    let json = serde_json::to_string(&kind).unwrap();
    assert_eq!(json, "\"bun_typecheck\"");
}

#[test]
fn test_bun_kinds_are_distinct() {
    assert_ne!(CompilationKind::BunTest, CompilationKind::BunTypecheck);
    assert_ne!(CompilationKind::BunTest, CompilationKind::CargoTest);
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] BunTest variant added to CompilationKind
- [ ] BunTypecheck variant added to CompilationKind
- [ ] Serialization produces snake_case names
- [ ] Deserialization works correctly
- [ ] All exhaustive matches updated (if any)
- [ ] cargo clippy passes
- [ ] cargo test passes

## Dependencies

- remote_compilation_helper-9ab (keywords must be in place first)

## Blocked By

- remote_compilation_helper-9ab (Add Bun to Keywords)

## Effort Estimate

Small - ~15 lines of code changes + tests","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T01:32:26.792287987-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T01:52:15.030528367-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T01:52:15.030528367-05:00","close_reason":"BunTest and BunTypecheck variants added to CompilationKind enum. Serialization tests pass. All 18 bun-related tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-aeq","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-9ab","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T01:33:06.282184618-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
-{"id":"remote_compilation_helper-ai1x","title":"Optimize artifact return for test-only commands (skip unnecessary target/ sync)","description":"## Context & Background

Currently, after remote execution, RCH retrieves artifacts via rsync:
```rust
// hook.rs line 691-718
let artifact_patterns = get_artifact_patterns(kind);
match pipeline.retrieve_artifacts(&worker_config, &artifact_patterns).await {
    ...
}
```

For tests, `get_artifact_patterns(Some(CompilationKind::CargoTest))` returns `default_rust_artifact_patterns()` which includes target/.

## Problem

For pure test runs:
1. Test output is streamed via stdout/stderr (already working)
2. Test binaries in target/debug/deps/ are NOT needed locally
3. Downloading target/ artifacts wastes bandwidth and time
4. Only exception: coverage files (target/llvm-cov-target/, etc.)

## Proposed Solution

1. Create test-specific artifact patterns:
```rust
pub fn default_rust_test_artifact_patterns() -> Vec<String> {
    vec![
        // Coverage files only (if coverage enabled)
        \"target/llvm-cov-target/**\".to_string(),
        \"target/coverage/**\".to_string(),
        // Test result files (if any)
        \"target/nextest/**\".to_string(),
    ]
}
```

2. Update get_artifact_patterns in hook.rs:
```rust
fn get_artifact_patterns(kind: Option<CompilationKind>) -> Vec<String> {
    match kind {
        Some(CompilationKind::CargoTest) => default_rust_test_artifact_patterns(),
        // ... rest
    }
}
```

3. Consider command analysis for coverage detection:
```rust
fn needs_coverage_artifacts(command: &str) -> bool {
    command.contains(\"--coverage\") || 
    command.contains(\"LLVM_PROFILE_FILE\") ||
    command.contains(\"cargo llvm-cov\")
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] cargo test does NOT download full target/ directory
- [ ] Coverage files ARE downloaded when coverage enabled
- [ ] Test execution performance improved (less artifact transfer time)
- [ ] Build commands still download target/ as before

## Performance Impact

For a typical Rust project:
- target/ can be 1-10GB
- Test artifacts needed: ~0 (just output)
- Coverage artifacts: ~10-100MB
- Expected speedup: Significant for test-only commands

## Files to Modify

- rch/src/hook.rs (get_artifact_patterns)
- rch/src/transfer.rs (default_rust_test_artifact_patterns)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T02:11:37.26784191-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T02:11:37.26784191-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ai1x","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-xcvl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T02:17:13.934065982-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

 {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-alo","title":"Improve error messages with actionable suggestions and help links","description":"## Overview

Improve error messages across CLI and hook paths with actionable, user-friendly guidance, structured error codes, and optional help links (OSC-8 when supported). Errors must be concise but informative.

This bead builds on the miette error diagnostics (remote_compilation_helper-gof) to provide rich, contextual error messages.

## Goals

1. Standardize error codes (rch::category::specific)
2. Add remediation hints (commands to run)
3. Include linkable docs where helpful (OSC-8 hyperlinks)
4. Support JSON error details
5. Show source context for config/code errors

## Error Categories

| Category | Code Prefix | Examples |
|----------|-------------|----------|
| Config | rch::config | invalid_toml, missing_field, permission |
| Worker | rch::worker | connection_failed, unhealthy, timeout |
| Daemon | rch::daemon | not_running, port_in_use, startup_failed |
| Transfer | rch::transfer | rsync_failed, ssh_auth, permission |
| Hook | rch::hook | classify_failed, parse_error |

## Error Message Format

### Human Output
```
Error: rch::worker::connection_failed

  × Connection to gpu-worker failed
   ╭────
   │ Could not establish SSH connection to build@192.168.1.100:22
   │ Reason: Permission denied (publickey)
   ╰────
  help: Verify SSH access with:
        ssh -i ~/.ssh/rch_key build@192.168.1.100

  docs: https://rch.dev/docs/troubleshooting#ssh-connection
```

### JSON Output
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "rch::worker::connection_failed",
    "message": "Connection to gpu-worker failed",
    "details": {
      "worker_id": "gpu-worker",
      "host": "192.168.1.100",
      "user": "build",
      "reason": "Permission denied (publickey)"
    },
    "suggestions": [
      "ssh -i ~/.ssh/rch_key build@192.168.1.100"
    ],
    "docs_url": "https://rch.dev/docs/troubleshooting#ssh-connection"
  }
}
```

## Implementation

### Error Type Design

```rust
use miette::{Diagnostic, SourceSpan};
use thiserror::Error;

#[derive(Error, Diagnostic, Debug)]
#[error("Connection to {worker_id} failed")]
#[diagnostic(
    code(rch::worker::connection_failed),
    help("Verify SSH access with: ssh -i {identity_file} {user}@{host}"),
    url("https://rch.dev/docs/troubleshooting#ssh-connection")
)]
pub struct ConnectionError {
    pub worker_id: String,
    pub host: String,
    pub user: String,
    pub identity_file: String,
    #[source]
    pub source: std::io::Error,
}
```

### Error to JSON Conversion

```rust
impl From<&dyn Diagnostic> for JsonError {
    fn from(diag: &dyn Diagnostic) -> Self {
        JsonError {
            code: diag.code().map(|c| c.to_string()),
            message: diag.to_string(),
            help: diag.help().map(|h| h.to_string()),
            url: diag.url().map(|u| u.to_string()),
        }
    }
}
```

### Common Error Patterns

```rust
// Daemon not running
#[derive(Error, Diagnostic, Debug)]
#[error("Daemon is not running")]
#[diagnostic(
    code(rch::daemon::not_running),
    help("Start the daemon with: rch daemon start")
)]
pub struct DaemonNotRunning;

// Config parse error with source context
#[derive(Error, Diagnostic, Debug)]
#[error("Invalid configuration")]
#[diagnostic(code(rch::config::invalid))]
pub struct ConfigError {
    #[source_code]
    pub src: miette::NamedSource<String>,
    #[label("error here")]
    pub span: SourceSpan,
    #[help]
    pub help: String,
}

// Worker timeout
#[derive(Error, Diagnostic, Debug)]
#[error("Worker {worker_id} timed out after {timeout_secs}s")]
#[diagnostic(
    code(rch::worker::timeout),
    help("Check worker connectivity: rch workers probe {worker_id}")
)]
pub struct WorkerTimeout {
    pub worker_id: String,
    pub timeout_secs: u64,
}
```

## Terminal Hyperlinks (OSC-8)

When terminal supports OSC-8 hyperlinks:

```rust
fn format_help_link(url: &str, text: &str) -> String {
    if supports_hyperlinks() {
        format!("\x1b]8;;{}\x1b\\{}\x1b]8;;\x1b\\", url, text)
    } else {
        format!("{} ({})", text, url)
    }
}
```

## Tests

- Unit: error to JSON mapping preserves all fields
- Unit: miette formatting includes all diagnostics
- Integration: sample failures produce expected hints
- E2E: simulate daemon missing, worker unreachable, config errors

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All public errors have error codes
- [ ] Errors include actionable suggestions
- [ ] Config errors show source context
- [ ] JSON errors include code + message + suggestions
- [ ] Help URLs use OSC-8 when supported
- [ ] Non-TTY output omits ANSI codes

## Dependencies

- miette integration (remote_compilation_helper-gof)
- JSON output (remote_compilation_helper-b9p)
- Terminal hyperlinks (remote_compilation_helper-20k)

## Logging

- E2E logs should include the exact error message + suggestions emitted for each simulated failure.

 {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-asw","title":"Epic: E2E Integration Tests with Detailed Logging","description":"## Background
E2E tests verify the complete RCH system as users experience it.

## Current Status: Tests PASSING ✅
All 17 E2E daemon tests pass consistently (verified 3 consecutive runs).
Previous failures were transient socket issues, now resolved.

## E2E Test Architecture
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                     Test Harness (rch-common::e2e)           │
│  - TestHarness: daemon lifecycle management                  │
│  - TestLogger: structured logging with timestamps            │
│  - Fixtures: workers, projects, configs                      │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
         │                    │                    │
    ┌────▼────┐         ┌────▼────┐         ┌────▼────┐
    │  rchd   │         │ rch CLI │         │ hook    │
    │ daemon  │◄────────│ commands│         │ classify│
    └────┬────┘         └─────────┘         └────┬────┘
         │                                        │
    ┌────▼────────────────────────────────────────▼────┐
    │              Transfer Pipeline (rsync)            │
    │  - Source sync to worker                          │
    │  - Artifact retrieval                             │
    └──────────────────────┬───────────────────────────┘
                           │
                    ┌──────▼──────┐
                    │   Worker    │
                    │ (SSH/Mock)  │
                    └─────────────┘
```

 {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-ayn","title":"Epic: Automatic Toolchain Version Synchronization","description":"## Overview

Implement automatic Rust toolchain synchronization between local machine and remote workers to eliminate version mismatch failures. This epic covers detection, transport, worker verification, caching, and robust testing.

## Goals

1. Detect local toolchain (channel/date/version)
2. Send toolchain info through daemon protocol
3. Ensure worker has toolchain (install if missing)
4. Execute remote commands with matching toolchain
5. Cache toolchain availability to avoid repeated checks
6. Fail‑open to local execution if sync fails

## Sub‑Beads

- Protocol + transfer support (remote_compilation_helper-o9s)
- Worker toolchain verification + install (remote_compilation_helper-0lo)
- Test coverage and E2E (remote_compilation_helper-mio)

## Testing Requirements

- Unit: toolchain detection + formatting
- Integration: mock worker install flow
- E2E: toolchain sync with mock SSH + failure injection

## Acceptance Criteria

- Worker uses exact toolchain as local
- Mismatch errors eliminated or surfaced with clear message
- Failures fall back to local
- Tests cover success + failure paths

## Dependencies

- Local fallback epic (remote_compilation_helper-ne8)

## Logging

- E2E logs must include local toolchain detection, worker toolchain ensure/install path, and any fall‑open decisions.

 {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-y9z","title":"E2E Tests: Worker Connectivity and Execution","description":"## Overview
E2E tests for worker connectivity, health checks, and remote execution.

## Test Categories

### 1. Connectivity Tests

#### test_worker_probe_success
```
[e2e::worker] TEST START: test_worker_probe_success
[e2e::worker] CONFIG: worker_id=test-1 host=localhost port=2222
[e2e::worker] PROBE: initiating SSH connection
[e2e::worker] PROBE: ssh_connected latency_ms=45
[e2e::worker] PROBE: running health check command
[e2e::worker] PROBE: health_response="OK rustc 1.75.0, cargo 1.75.0"
[e2e::worker] RESULT: status=healthy slots_available=8
[e2e::worker] TEST PASS: test_worker_probe_success
```

#### test_worker_probe_failure
- Unreachable host → clear error
- Wrong port → connection refused
- Auth failure → permission denied

#### test_worker_probe_timeout
- Slow host → timeout after 10s
- Verify: Timeout doesn't hang test

### 2. Circuit Breaker Tests

#### test_worker_circuit_breaker_opens
```
[e2e::worker] TEST START: test_worker_circuit_breaker_opens
[e2e::worker] CONFIG: worker=bad-worker threshold=3
[e2e::worker] ATTEMPT: 1 result=connection_refused
[e2e::worker] CIRCUIT: failures=1/3 state=CLOSED
[e2e::worker] ATTEMPT: 2 result=connection_refused  
[e2e::worker] CIRCUIT: failures=2/3 state=CLOSED
[e2e::worker] ATTEMPT: 3 result=connection_refused
[e2e::worker] CIRCUIT: failures=3/3 state=OPEN
[e2e::worker] ATTEMPT: 4 result=circuit_open (no connection attempted)
[e2e::worker] VERIFY: state=OPEN rejection_time_ms=0
[e2e::worker] TEST PASS: test_worker_circuit_breaker_opens
```

#### test_worker_circuit_breaker_half_open
- After timeout → HALF_OPEN state
- Single probe attempt allowed
- Success → CLOSED, Failure → OPEN

#### test_worker_circuit_breaker_recovery
- Full cycle: CLOSED → OPEN → HALF_OPEN → CLOSED
- Verify state transitions logged

### 3. Command Execution Tests

#### test_remote_command_success
```
[e2e::worker] TEST START: test_remote_command_success
[e2e::worker] WORKER: test-1
[e2e::worker] COMMAND: echo "hello world"
[e2e::worker] EXECUTE: ssh test-1 'echo "hello world"'
[e2e::worker] STDOUT: hello world
[e2e::worker] STDERR: (empty)
[e2e::worker] EXIT: code=0 duration_ms=123
[e2e::worker] TEST PASS: test_remote_command_success
```

#### test_remote_command_failure
- Command returns non-zero
- Verify: Exit code captured

#### test_remote_command_timeout
- Long-running command
- Verify: Killed after timeout

#### test_remote_command_output_streaming
- Large output (>1MB)
- Verify: Streamed, not buffered

### 4. Worker Selection Tests

#### test_worker_selection_single
- One worker available
- Verify: Selected immediately

#### test_worker_selection_multiple
```
[e2e::worker] TEST START: test_worker_selection_multiple
[e2e::worker] WORKERS: [gpu-1 (slots=8, priority=100), cpu-1 (slots=4, priority=50)]
[e2e::worker] SELECTION: scoring workers...
[e2e::worker] SCORE: gpu-1 = 8 * 100 * 1.0 = 800
[e2e::worker] SCORE: cpu-1 = 4 * 50 * 1.0 = 200
[e2e::worker] SELECTED: gpu-1 (highest score)
[e2e::worker] TEST PASS: test_worker_selection_multiple
```

#### test_worker_selection_all_busy
- No available slots
- Verify: Returns no-worker with reason

#### test_worker_selection_tag_filtering
- Project requires ["rust", "fast"]
- Verify: Only matching workers selected

### 5. SSH Authentication Tests

#### test_ssh_key_auth
- Ed25519, RSA, ECDSA keys
- Verify: All key types work

#### test_ssh_agent_auth
- SSH_AUTH_SOCK set
- Verify: Agent forwarding works

### 6. Edge Cases

#### test_worker_reconnect_after_network_blip
#### test_worker_handles_utf8_output
#### test_worker_handles_binary_output

| Command Execution | 4 |

 {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-ya16","title":"Handle test filtering and special flags (--ignored, --include-ignored, --exact)","description":"## Context & Background

cargo test supports various filtering and flag options that affect execution:

- `cargo test test_name` - Run tests matching pattern
- `cargo test -- --ignored` - Run only ignored tests
- `cargo test -- --include-ignored` - Run all including ignored
- `cargo test -- --exact` - Exact match for test name
- `cargo test -- --skip pattern` - Skip tests matching pattern
- `cargo test -- --show-output` - Show stdout for passing tests

These flags affect:
1. Which tests run (affects duration)
2. Slot requirements (fewer tests = fewer slots needed)
3. Output verbosity

## Current State

Classification handles `cargo test` generically without parsing these flags.
Slot estimation is fixed at 4 regardless of test count.

## Proposed Solution

### 1. Parse test filter for slot estimation
```rust
fn estimate_test_slots(command: &str) -> u32 {
    // Check for specific test names (likely fewer tests)
    if command.contains(\" -- \") {
        // Has test args, might be filtered
        let after_sep = command.split(\" -- \").nth(1).unwrap_or(\"\");
        if !after_sep.starts_with('-') {
            // Has test name filter
            return 2; // Fewer slots for filtered tests
        }
    }
    // Check for --ignored (usually fewer tests)
    if command.contains(\"--ignored\") {
        return 4;
    }
    8 // Default for full test suite
}
```

### 2. Handle --nocapture for output
```rust
// --nocapture means we'll see test stdout
// Ensure streaming handles this properly
if command.contains(\"--nocapture\") || command.contains(\"--show-output\") {
    // Expect more verbose output
}
```

### 3. Consider --test-threads parsing
```rust
fn parse_test_threads(command: &str) -> Option<u32> {
    // cargo test -- --test-threads=4
    let re = regex::Regex::new(r\"--test-threads[=\s](\d+)\").ok()?;
    re.captures(command)
        .and_then(|c| c.get(1))
        .and_then(|m| m.as_str().parse().ok())
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Filtered tests (with name pattern) use fewer slots
- [ ] --test-threads flag respected in slot estimation
- [ ] --nocapture output streams correctly
- [ ] --ignored tests handled without issues

## Files to Modify

- rch/src/hook.rs (slot estimation)
- rch-common/src/patterns.rs (if classification needs update)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T02:13:49.007417317-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T02:13:49.007417317-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ya16","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-yj8b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T02:17:24.879178843-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-yj4","title":"Implement rch-wkr binary auto-deployment","description":"## Overview
Sub-task of worker provisioning: automatically deploy the rch-wkr binary to remote workers.

## Background
Manual deployment required:
1. scp binary to home dir (can't write directly to /usr/local/bin)
2. ssh to run 'sudo mv ~/rch-wkr /usr/local/bin/'
3. Verify with 'rch-wkr health'

## Requirements
1. Check if rch-wkr is already installed (and version matches)
2. If not, transfer binary via scp/rsync
3. Handle permission elevation (sudo) for /usr/local/bin
4. Fall back to ~/bin or ~/.local/bin if no sudo
5. Verify installation via health check
6. Track deployed version for update management

## Technical Approach
- Check current version: ssh worker 'rch-wkr --version 2>/dev/null || echo "not-installed"'
- Transfer to temp location: scp binary user@host:~/rch-wkr.tmp
- Atomic install: ssh 'sudo mv ~/rch-wkr.tmp /usr/local/bin/rch-wkr && chmod +x /usr/local/bin/rch-wkr'
- Verify: ssh 'rch-wkr health'

## Considerations
- Binary architecture must match worker (x86_64 vs aarch64)
- Cross-compilation for heterogeneous fleets
- Bandwidth optimization: compress during transfer
- Rollback: keep previous version as rch-wkr.bak

## Success Criteria
- rch workers deploy-binary css installs rch-wkr
- Skips if already at correct version
- Works without sudo using fallback paths","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T02:16:32.5201063-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T03:30:37.837958385-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T03:30:37.837958385-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented workers deploy-binary command: finds local binary, checks remote version, deploys via SCP to ~/.local/bin, makes executable, verifies with health check"}
-{"id":"remote_compilation_helper-yj8b","title":"Implement test-aware slot estimation based on RUST_TEST_THREADS","description":"## Context & Background

Currently, slot estimation is hardcoded to 4 cores in hook.rs line 217:
```rust
let estimated_cores = 4;
```

This is suboptimal for tests because:
1. `cargo test` runs test binaries in parallel (default = num CPUs via RUST_TEST_THREADS)
2. Each test binary may also run tests in parallel
3. Tests can be CPU-intensive (especially integration tests)

## Problem

- Build commands compile incrementally, parallelism controlled by -j flag
- Test commands run test binaries in parallel based on RUST_TEST_THREADS
- Using fixed 4 cores underestimates test parallelism needs
- Could lead to worker oversubscription during test runs

## Proposed Solution

1. Add CompilationKind-aware slot estimation in hook.rs:
```rust
fn estimate_cores_for_kind(kind: Option<CompilationKind>, command: &str) -> u32 {
    match kind {
        Some(CompilationKind::CargoTest) => {
            // Check for --test-threads= or RUST_TEST_THREADS
            // Default: request more slots for parallel test execution
            if command.contains(\"--test-threads=1\") {
                2 // Single-threaded tests need less
            } else {
                8 // Default: request more for parallel tests
            }
        }
        Some(CompilationKind::CargoBuild | CompilationKind::CargoCheck) => {
            // Parse -j flag if present, otherwise default
            parse_jobs_flag(command).unwrap_or(4)
        }
        _ => 4 // Default
    }
}
```

2. Consider making this configurable in config.toml:
```toml
[compilation]
default_slots_build = 4
default_slots_test = 8
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Slot estimation varies by CompilationKind
- [ ] cargo test requests more slots than cargo build by default
- [ ] --test-threads flag parsing reduces slot request appropriately
- [ ] -j flag parsing works for build commands
- [ ] Configuration override available

## Files to Modify

- rch/src/hook.rs (estimate_cores_for_kind function)
- rch-common/src/config.rs (add slot estimation config)
- rch-common/src/types.rs (if needed)","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T02:11:35.804872712-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T03:51:38.612139493-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-yj8b","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-xcvl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T02:17:12.684708682-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

 {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-ytp","title":"Implement toolchain synchronization across workers","description":"## Overview
Sub-task of worker provisioning: ensure workers have the required Rust toolchains installed and available.

## Background
The dogfooding session revealed workers had different Rust versions:
- css: rustc 1.92.0 (stable)
- csd: rustc 1.94.0-nightly

But the project required nightly-2025-01-01. This caused initial build failure until manually fixed.

## Requirements
1. Detect project toolchain from rust-toolchain.toml
2. Check each worker's installed toolchains
3. Install missing toolchains via rustup
4. Handle case where rustup itself isn't installed
5. Optionally set the required toolchain as default
6. Support toolchain override via environment

## Technical Approach
1. Parse rust-toolchain.toml for channel/version
2. SSH to worker: 'rustup show' to list installed toolchains
3. If missing: 'rustup install <toolchain>'
4. Optionally: 'rustup default <toolchain>'

## Edge Cases
- Worker has no Rust at all (needs rustup installation first)
- Worker behind corporate proxy (rustup download issues)
- Custom toolchain directories
- nightly vs specific date (nightly-2025-01-01)
- Multiple projects with different toolchain requirements

## Dependency on rust-toolchain.toml
The project's rust-toolchain.toml specifies:
```toml
[toolchain]
channel = "nightly-2025-01-01"
```

This must be synced to all workers before compilation can succeed.

## Success Criteria
- rch workers sync-toolchain css installs required toolchain
- rch workers sync-toolchain --all handles entire fleet
- Clear error if rustup not installed (with instructions)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T02:16:33.943622903-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T03:38:10.830534971-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T03:38:10.830534971-05:00","close_reason":"Toolchain synchronization fully implemented and tested: detect_project_toolchain(), sync_toolchain_to_worker(), check_remote_toolchain(), install_remote_toolchain() all working. 64 tests passing. Command 'rch workers sync-toolchain --all' successfully detects required toolchain and syncs to workers."}
 {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-zerp","title":"Handle cargo test exit code semantics correctly (101 = test failures vs 1 = build error)","description":"## Context & Background

Cargo test has specific exit code semantics:
- Exit 0: All tests passed
- Exit 1: Build/compilation error (couldn't compile tests)
- Exit 101: Tests ran but some failed

## Current State

Looking at hook.rs lines 294-340, exit code handling is:
```rust
if result.exit_code == 0 {
    // Success - deny local
} else if is_toolchain_failure(&result.stderr, exit_code) {
    // Toolchain issue - allow local fallback
} else {
    // Failed - deny local
}
```

This treats all non-zero as "failed remotely, deny local" which IS correct for tests!

## Verification Needed

1. Confirm exit 101 (test failures) is handled correctly
   - Should: Deny local (agent already saw failures, re-running won't help)
   - Current: Denies - CORRECT

2. Confirm exit 1 (build error) is handled correctly
   - Should: Deny local (same build error would occur locally)
   - Current: Denies - CORRECT

3. Confirm exit 1 with toolchain error allows fallback
   - Should: Allow local (might have toolchain locally)
   - Current: Checks is_toolchain_failure() - CORRECT

## Edge Cases to Consider

### 1. cargo test --no-run
- Only compiles test binaries, doesn't run
- Exit 0 = compiled, Exit 1 = build error
- Should be handled same as cargo build

### 2. Test timeouts
- Individual test timeouts produce test failures
- Overall SSH timeout is different
- Need to distinguish

### 3. Signal exits
- SIGKILL/SIGTERM produce exit codes 128+signal
- May indicate resource issues on worker
- Consider fallback for these?

## Proposed Solution

### 1. Add exit code documentation
```rust
/// Exit codes for cargo test:
/// - 0: All tests passed
/// - 1: Build/compilation error
/// - 101: One or more tests failed
/// - 128+N: Killed by signal N
const CARGO_TEST_ALL_PASSED: i32 = 0;
const CARGO_TEST_BUILD_ERROR: i32 = 1;
const CARGO_TEST_FAILURES: i32 = 101;
```

### 2. Consider signal-killed handling
```rust
fn is_signal_killed(exit_code: i32) -> bool {
    exit_code > 128
}

// In handle_result:
if is_signal_killed(result.exit_code) {
    warn!(\"Remote command killed by signal {}\", result.exit_code - 128);
    // Consider: should we fallback to local?
    // Probably not - signal kill likely means OOM or similar
}
```

### 3. Add more specific exit code logging
```rust
if result.exit_code == 101 {
    info!(\"Tests failed (exit 101), denying local re-execution\");
} else if result.exit_code == 1 {
    info!(\"Build error (exit 1), denying local re-execution\");
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Exit 0 (success) denies local - VERIFY
- [ ] Exit 101 (test failures) denies local - VERIFY
- [ ] Exit 1 (build error) denies local - VERIFY
- [ ] Toolchain failures allow local fallback - VERIFY
- [ ] Signal kills logged appropriately
- [ ] Exit codes documented in code

## Files to Modify

- rch/src/hook.rs (exit code handling documentation and logging)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T02:15:58.687574266-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T02:54:31.668999541-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-18T02:54:31.668999541-05:00","close_reason":"## Already Fully Implemented ✅

All acceptance criteria were already met in the codebase:

### 1. Exit Code Constants (hook.rs:50-58)
```rust
const EXIT_SUCCESS: i32 = 0;
const EXIT_BUILD_ERROR: i32 = 1;
const EXIT_TEST_FAILURES: i32 = 101;
const EXIT_SIGNAL_BASE: i32 = 128;
```

### 2. Specific Exit Code Handling (hook.rs:369-408)
- Signal-killed (128+N): Logs with signal name (SIGKILL, SIGTERM, etc.), denies local
- Test failures (101): "Remote tests failed (exit 101) on {worker}, denying local re-execution"
- Build error (1): "Remote build error (exit 1) on {worker}, denying local re-execution"
- Other non-zero: Generic handling with exit code

### 3. Signal Detection Helpers (hook.rs:631-652)
- `is_signal_killed(exit_code)` - returns Some(signal) if > 128
- `signal_name(signal)` - SIGHUP, SIGINT, SIGKILL, SIGSEGV, SIGTERM, etc.

### 4. Documentation in Code (hook.rs:362-365)
Inline comments explain all exit code semantics

### 5. Tests (hook.rs:1920-1971)
- test_signal_name ✓
- test_exit_code_constants ✓
- test_is_toolchain_failure_basic ✓
- test_exit_code_semantics_documented ✓
- test_process_hook_remote_nonzero_exit_denies ✓

All 38 hook tests pass. No code changes needed."}
